title: Z-Score Calculator – Standard Score
shortDescription: Calculate the z-score (standard score) of a value.
description: Enter a value, the mean, and the standard deviation to get the z-score. Runs in your browser — no signup.
metaDescription: Free z-score calculator. Enter a value, the mean, and the standard deviation to get the standard score using z = (x − μ) / σ, instantly in your browser.

result.label: Z-score
result.empty: Enter value, mean, and standard deviation.

sections.heading: What is a z-score?
paragraphs: A z-score, or standard score, tells you how many standard deviations a value is from the mean. It is calculated as z = (x − μ) / σ, where x is the value, μ is the mean, and σ is the standard deviation. A z-score of 0 equals the mean; +1 is one standard deviation above it.

sections.heading: Interpreting the result
paragraphs: Positive z-scores are above the mean, negative ones below. In a normal distribution, about 68% of values fall within ±1, 95% within ±2, and 99.7% within ±3. Z-scores let you compare values from different datasets on the same scale.

howTo.name: Enter the value
howTo.text: Type the value x you want to standardize.

howTo.name: Enter mean and SD
howTo.text: Type the mean and the standard deviation.

howTo.name: Read the z-score
howTo.text: See how many standard deviations x is from the mean.

faq.a: z = (x − μ) / σ — the value minus the mean, divided by the standard deviation.

faq.q: What does a negative z-score mean?
faq.a: The value is below the mean. A z-score of −1.5 is 1.5 standard deviations below average.

faq.q: What is a good or unusual z-score?
faq.a: Values beyond ±2 or ±3 are uncommon in a normal distribution and are often treated as outliers.

faq.q: Can the standard deviation be zero?
faq.a: No. Dividing by zero is undefined, so the SD must be greater than zero.

faq.q: Is the z-score the same as a percentile?
faq.a: Related but not identical. A z-score maps to a percentile via the normal distribution's cumulative function.
